title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Restore · Contract Lens: Versioning State Transition
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing immutable-retention and recovery runbook; use version-specific branded identities, irreversible retention confirmation, recovery commands behind authorization, and executed restore evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the versioning state transition increment in immutable-retention and recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Versioning State Transition · Theory Versioning State Transi…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5226 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow versioning state and require version-specific retention and restore evidence; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Versioning State Transition contract for the supplied data.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Versioning State Transition model · Theory Versioning Sta…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Suspending versioning returns a bucket to its original unversioned behavior. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
